title: Kiriteswari Temple
tagline: Kiriteswari Temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Kiriteswari Temple is situated in Kiritkona village under the Nabagram CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the state of West Bengal.
overview.description: Kiriteswari Temple is situated in Kiritkona village under the Nabagram CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the state of West Bengal. This is one of the Shakta pithas among the 51 pithas. In September 2023 this area was selected as the best tourism village of India.

nearestAirport.name: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jafarganj Cemetery
topPlaces.description: Jafarganj Cemetery is located in Murshidabad, West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Namak Haram Deorhi
topPlaces.description: Namak Haram Deorhi (Bengali: নিমোক হারাম দেওরহি, lit. 'Traitor's Gate'), originally known as Jafragunj Deohri, was the palace of Mir Jafar. It is located in Murshidabad, just opposite to the Jafarganj Cemetery in the Lalbagh area, in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Tomb of Azimunissa Begum
topPlaces.description: Tomb of Azimunissa Begum is located at Azimnagar (oid Murshidabad), in the Murshidabad district. According to the List of Monuments of National Importance in West Bengal the Tomb of Azimunissa Begum is an ASI Listed Monument.

topPlaces.name: Rosnaiganj
topPlaces.description: Rosnaiganj is a village in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Kathgola
topPlaces.description: Kathgola (also known as Katgola) is a neighbourhood in the city of Murshidabad which was at one time the capital of Bengal, Bihar and Orissa during the reign of the Nawabs of Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Kathgola Palace
topPlaces.description: Kathgola Palace is a historical building belonging to the Dugar family at Kathgola in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block of Murshidabad district. It now houses a museum.

topPlaces.name: 2010 West Bengal ferry sinking
topPlaces.description: The 2010 West Bengal Ferry Sinking was an incident which occurred on Saturday, 30 October 2010, when an over-crowded ferry carrying Muslim pilgrims sank after hitting a sand bank on the Bhagirathi River in the Indian state of West Bengal. Thus far at least 74 bodies have been recovered, with many more missing, feared swept downstream.

topPlaces.name: Yellow Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Yellow Mosque, also known as the Zurud Mosque, is a mosque located in the Hazarduari Palace complex at Murshidabad, in the state of West Bengal, India. The Yellow Mosque was built by Nawab Siraj ud-Daulah in 1756–57, in the Hazarduari Palace complex, on the banks of the Bhagirathi River.

items.note: Jafarganj Cemetery is located in Murshidabad, West Bengal, India.

Namak Haram Deorhi (Bengali: নিমোক হারাম দেওরহি, lit. 'Traitor's Gate'), originally known as Jafragunj Deohri, was the palace of Mir Jafar. It is located in Murshidabad, just opposite to the Jafarganj Cemetery in the Lalbagh area, in the Indian state of West Bengal.

Tomb of Azimunissa Begum is located at Azimnagar (oid Murshidabad), in the Murshidabad district. According to the List of Monuments of National Importance in West Bengal the Tomb of Azimunissa Begum is an ASI Listed Monument.
Rosnaiganj is a village in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the state of West Bengal, India.
Kathgola (also known as Katgola) is a neighbourhood in the city of Murshidabad which was at one time the capital of Bengal, Bihar and Orissa during the reign of the Nawabs of Bengal.
Kathgola Palace is a historical building belonging to the Dugar family at Kathgola in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block of Murshidabad district. It now houses a museum.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — 2010 West Bengal ferry sinking & around

items.note: The 2010 West Bengal Ferry Sinking was an incident which occurred on Saturday, 30 October 2010, when an over-crowded ferry carrying Muslim pilgrims sank after hitting a sand bank on the Bhagirathi River in the Indian state of West Bengal. Thus far at least 74 bodies have been recovered, with many more missing, feared swept downstream.

The Yellow Mosque, also known as the Zurud Mosque, is a mosque located in the Hazarduari Palace complex at Murshidabad, in the state of West Bengal, India. The Yellow Mosque was built by Nawab Siraj ud-Daulah in 1756–57, in the Hazarduari Palace complex, on the banks of the Bhagirathi River.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kiriteswari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kiriteswari Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kiriteswari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (173 km) and the nearest railway station is Katihar Junction (162 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kiriteswari Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kiriteswari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kiriteswari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kiriteswari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kiriteswari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jafarganj Cemetery, Namak Haram Deorhi, Tomb of Azimunissa Begum, Rosnaiganj, Kathgola.
